Kaufman House
Section 01
Welcome

The ultimate goal at Juliette Calaf Interiors is for you to enjoy a collaborative and stress-free design experience that results in a beautiful finished project. Our rigorous design sensibility and comprehensive process allows us to create a space that tells your unique story and serves as the backdrop for the life you live in it.

We specialize in creating spaces with presence, grounded in proportion, architectural balance, and a deep respect for craftsmanship. The result is a quiet, confident aesthetic that feels collected and uniquely yours.

Our work has been featured in the Wall Street Journal, Traditional Home, Business of Home, Modo de Vida, and several other publications.

"Our role is to understand how you use the space and translate that into an environment that feels natural, refined, and built to endure."

Juliette Calaf, Creative Director
www.juliettecalaf.com
Style Your Story
juliette CALAF interiors
Kaufman House
Section 02
About This Project
Architectural rendering · Diez Mil Siete Arquitectura

Your home sits on Park Boulevard in San Juan, where two adjoining lots become a single fifteen-thousand-square-foot residence designed around the ocean view. Because you have lived in the existing structure, you already know how you want to move through it. That is a rare starting point, and it makes this the right moment to design: while the architecture is still taking shape with Alexandra and the team at Diez Mil Siete, the decisions that matter most are still open.

The current layout works against the way your family lives. It was built as two separate apartments, each with its own kitchen and a back entrance that was never meant to be the front door. The work ahead is to bring it together into one coherent home, one that functions for a growing family with a baby on the way and more planned, for older children who visit, and for the life you actually want to live in it, without losing what already works.

The aesthetic direction is earthy and layered, never monotone and never all beige. Olive and aubergine, warm woods, raw textures, a few unexpected pieces that hold a conversation. Tokyo meets Paris, grounded in the salt-air reality of San Juan, where materials have to earn their place. Teak, ipe, and cumaru over metals that rust in the sea air.

Our role is to design alongside your architect from the start, so that the way you actually live, where the children sleep, where the dogs drink, where the hammock hangs, is built into the bones of the house rather than solved after the fact.

www.juliettecalaf.com
Style Your Story
juliette CALAF interiors
Kaufman House
Section 03
Scope by Room

We have heard your wish to bring this investment closer to your target, and we have shaped the scope around it, holding on to the spaces that matter most to design now and setting the simpler furnishing rooms aside for whenever you are ready. There is a quiet logic to doing the heart of the home in one coordinated effort rather than returning a year or two later to begin again. It protects your timeline, and it protects your budget.

What we offer is not design alone. It is a home made whole, considered to a level that is genuinely unparalleled, every detail drawn, specified, and resolved down to the linens on the bed. Nothing left to chance, nothing left for later. When the work is done, the whole household, baby and dogs included, walks into a home that is finished, styled, and waiting, ready to live in from the very first day.

The areas outlined in yellow on the plans that follow are the spaces included in this proposal.

Ground Floor

The ground floor carries the everyday life of the house. We design the new living area and the existing living room as warm, connected gathering spaces, with the gym, sauna, laundry, and service rooms detailed for how the family actually moves through the day. The central atrium ties the level together as a single, considered volume.

Ground floor · areas outlined in yellow are included in this scope
www.juliettecalaf.com
Style Your Story
juliette CALAF interiors
Kaufman House
Second Floor

The second floor is where the house entertains and rests in equal measure. We design the bar and lounge, the dining room, and the kitchen as a flowing sequence built for gathering, with the balcony treated as a true extension of the living space. The bedrooms on this level are detailed as calm, private retreats, finished to the same standard as the rest of the home.

Second floor · areas outlined in yellow are included in this scope
www.juliettecalaf.com
Style Your Story
juliette CALAF interiors
Kaufman House
Third Floor

The third floor is the private heart of the home. The master suite, with its bath and closet, is designed as a quiet retreat that keeps the light and the view while protecting the sense of seclusion. The study, the children's rooms, and their bathrooms are planned with the same care, so the whole level feels considered, restful, and built to grow with the family. The two children's rooms are designed with the future in mind, planned so they can later convert into a media room, yoga studio, or whatever the household needs, without a second design effort.

Third floor · areas outlined in yellow are included in this scope

Section 04
Design Fee

Your design fee is a flat fee. It covers the full creative scope of the project: planning, selections, specifications, sourcing and procurement of everything that goes into the home. There is no separate charge for purchasing. Our compensation on procurement comes through the items we source and sell.

The fee is calculated room by room, based on the work each space requires. Rooms that involve any element of construction, meaning anything beyond furniture, wallpaper, and accessories, are priced at $36 per square foot. Rooms that are furnishing and styling only are priced at $18 per square foot. As the project unfolds, scope can be assessed and adjusted along the way, so if a furnished room later calls for construction or built-ins, it moves to the $36 rate by simple change order.

Should you choose to add rooms or scope once the project is underway, that additional work is billed by the hour at the rates below, by simple change order. Project management, the hands-on coordination of the build and installation, is also billed by the hour: $225 per hour (Principal) and $175 per hour (Design Staff), invoiced monthly in quarter-hour increments. You will be working closely with Brian Tenenbaum throughout construction.

Payment Schedule

This is a Flat Fee Agreement, structured in two parts tied to what you receive.

50%
To Begin
Due on signing. This reserves your place in our calendar and opens Phase 1, where we study the space and shape the concept. Non-refundable.
50%
To See It Come Together
Due as we move into design development, when the full vision for your home is presented to you for the first time.

All fees are exclusive of FF&E, taxes, shipping, and logistics. Managed services billed at cost plus 20%. Reimbursable expenses at cost plus 10%.

Section 06
Our Process & What Is Included
1
Conceptual Development
Study of existing conditions, space planning, mood boards, floor plans, and design concepts. Construction timeline and aesthetic direction established, in coordination with your architect.
2
Design Development
Specification of all finishes, fixtures, furnishings, window coverings, decorative lighting, and custom millwork direction. First design presentation held.
3
Procurement
Purchasing proposals issued for your approval. No item ordered without a signed proposal and required payment. Vendor coordination and logistics managed by JCI.
4
Installation
Coordination of delivery, installation, and final styling. Two installation days included in the flat fee. Site visits attended as needed and billed hourly under project management.
  • Full creative direction across all four service phases
  • Space planning, mood boards, floor plans, and ceiling plans
  • Finish, fixture, and FF&E selection and specification
  • Custom millwork direction and fabricator coordination
  • Procurement management and vendor coordination
  • FF&E logistics and storage coordination
  • Two installation days included in the flat fee
  • Final styling and accessory curation
Not included: Permit applications, structural or engineering services, construction documents, and MEP coordination.

Estimated timeline varies based on scope. Full-scope projects with custom fabrication typically run 18 to 24 months. A detailed project schedule will be provided at kickoff.*
* Timeline depends on scope, client approvals, vendor lead times, and construction schedule.
